Enables/Disables given HIOP STACK. Default is AUTO no stack is disabled. 1 - The stacks indicated by the bit locations are disabled. 0 - The stacks indicated by the bit locations are not modified. The stack order is abstracted each bit 0 = stack 0 ... bit n = stack n. The bit setting for each stack can be overriden by BIOS based on part-knob compatibility. CXL Security Level
0: Fully Trusted - CXL Device can get access on CXL.$ for host-attached and device attached memory ranges in the WB address space.
1: Partially Trusted - CXL Device can get access on CXL.$ for device attached memory ranges only;
2: Untrusted - All requests on CXL.$ will be aborted by the Host.
3: Auto - Currently identical to Fully Trusted. (Default) 0:Fully Trusted, 1:Partially Trusted, 2:Untrusted, 3:Auto

options.label: Auto - Currently identical to Fully Trusted

C1E - 1: Enable(Default), 0: Disable. 0:Disabled, 1:Enabled
Intel SST-PP Select allows user to choose level - 0xFF: Choose lowest level hardware supported(Default), 0: Level 0, 3: Level 3, 4: Level 4. 0xFF: AUTO (default), 0: Level 0, 3: Level 3, 4: Level 4
SST-BF - 0: Disable (Default), 1: Enabled. 0:Disabled, 1:Enabled
Set bit(s) to Disable or clear bit(s) to Enable core(s) on Socket 0. NOTE: Any core disabled by user will force static SST-PP.
Set bit(s) to Disable or clear bit(s) to Enable core(s) on Socket 1. NOTE: Any core disabled by user will force static SST-PP.
Processor TurboMode Function - 1: Enable(Default), 0: Disable Processor TurboMode Function. 0: Disable, 1: Enable
Processor Safer Mode Extensions Function - 0: Disable(Default), 1: Enable Processor Safer Mode Extensions Function. 0: Disable, 1: Enable

options.label: Enable Processor Safer Mode Extensions Function

parameters.description: SstCp - 1: Enable, 0: Disable SstCp(Default). 0: Disable, 1: Enable
SST-CP Capable Status in system - 0: Disable(Default), 1: Enable. 0: Disable, 1: Enable
Left shifted address by 12 bits with address bits 11:0 are assumed to be FFFh for limit comparison.
Left shifted address by 12 bits with address bits 11:0 are assumed to be 0.
